figures.caption: Figure 1: Illustration of existing ICL paradigms (top) and challenges (bottom) in EHR-based clinical reasoning.

Table 5: Statistics of the MIMIC-III dataset and MIMIC-IV dataset. “Re.” stands for Readmission, indicating patients who are readmitted to the ICU within 30 days of discharge, while “No Re.” represents patients who are not readmitted.
Figure 8: Shot scaling performance on MIMIC-IV Readmission with Qwen3-14B as the backbone LLM.
Table 10: Key Notations Used in GraphWalker
Table 9: Case study on MIMIC-III Mortality.

Table 8: Results of the black-box variant of GraphWalker on MIMIC-III Mortality using GPT-5.2, and its compatibility with ReAct-style reasoning. ReAct uses the same few-shot demonstrations but adopts a more dynamic reasoning scaffold. Higher values indicate better performance (↑).

Figure 9: Performance–latency tradeoff on MIMIC-III Mortality with Qwen3-14B. The x-axis denotes the average inference time per case, and the y-axis shows AUROC, AUPRC, and F1, respectively. Each point represents a demonstration selection method, and the dashed line marks the Pareto frontier. GraphWalker lies on the Pareto frontier, indicating that an additional test-time cost of only several seconds per case, which is acceptable in many clinical decision-support scenarios, can yield substantial gains in predictive performance.

Table 1: Performance comparison on MIMIC-III and MIMIC-IV datasets using Qwen3-14B and Llama3.1-8B-Instruct as backbone LLMs. All evaluation metrics are defined such that higher values indicate better performance (↑). The best results are highlighted in bold, while the second-best results are underlined. Impro(%) indicates the relative improvement of GraphWalker over the second-best performance.

Table 2: Ablation study of GraphWalker using Qwen3-14B as backbone LLM on MIMIC-III and MIMIC-IV datasets. All evaluation metrics are defined such that higher values indicate better performance (↑). The best results are highlighted in Bold.
Figure 4: Analysis of Information Aggregation of Multiple Demonstrations using various Demonstration Selection Algorithm.

Table 6: Additional results on MIMIC-III and MIMIC-IV datasets using Qwen3-32B as the backbone LLM. All evaluation metrics are defined such that higher values indicate better performance (↑). The best results are highlighted in bold, while the second-best results are underlined. Impro(%) indicates the relative improvement of GraphWalker over the second-best performance.
